1162	        return engine_base.wrap_completion_response(
1163	            request_id=request_id,
1164	            model=model,
1165	            output_texts=output_texts,
1166	            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
1167	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1168	            usage=request_final_usage,
1169	        )

1171	    async def _handle_chat_completion(
1172	        self,
1173	        request: openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest,
1174	        request_id: str,
1175	        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
1176	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any]:
1177	        """The implementation fo asynchronous ChatCompletionRequest handling.
1178	
1179	        Yields
1180	        ------
1181	        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
1182	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1183	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1184	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.
1185	
1186	        Raises
1187	        ------
1188	        e : BadRequestError
1189	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1190	        """
1191	        (
1192	            prompts,
1193	            generation_cfg,
1194	            use_function_calling,
1195	            prompt_length,
1196	        ) = engine_base.process_chat_completion_request(
1197	            request,
1198	            request_id,
1199	            self.state,
1200	            self.model_config_dicts[0],
1201	            self.tokenizer.encode,
1202	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1203	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1204	        )
1205	        # prompt length is not used
1206	        _ = prompt_length
1207	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1208	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1209	        try:
1210	            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
1211	                prompts,
1212	                generation_cfg,
1213	                request_id,
1214	            ):
1215	                response = engine_base.process_chat_completion_stream_output(
1216	                    delta_outputs,
1217	                    request,
1218	                    request_id,
1219	                    self.state,
1220	                    use_function_calling,
1221	                    finish_reasons,
1222	                )
1223	
1224	                if response is not None:
1225	                    if response.usage is not None:
1226	                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
1227	                            response.usage.extra = None
1228	                    yield response
1229	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1230	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
1231	            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1232	            raise
1233	        except Exception as err:
1234	            logger.error("Error in _handle_chat_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
1235	            raise

1237	    async def _handle_completion(
1238	        self,
1239	        request: openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest,
1240	        request_id: str,
1241	        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
1242	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse, Any]:
1243	        """The implementation fo asynchronous CompletionRequest handling.
1244	
1245	        Yields
1246	        ------
1247	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1248	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1249	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1250	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/object for specification.
1251	
1252	        Raises
1253	        ------
1254	        e : BadRequestError
1255	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1256	        """
1257	        (
1258	            prompt,
1259	            generation_cfg,
1260	            prompt_length,
1261	            echo_response,
1262	        ) = engine_base.process_completion_request(
1263	            request,
1264	            request_id,
1265	            self.state,
1266	            self.tokenizer,
1267	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1268	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1269	        )
1270	        _ = prompt_length
1271	        if echo_response is not None:
1272	            yield echo_response
1273	
1274	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * generation_cfg.n  # noqa: UP006
1275	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1276	        try:
1277	            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
1278	                prompt,
1279	                generation_cfg,
1280	                request_id,
1281	            ):
1282	                response = engine_base.process_completion_stream_output(
1283	                    delta_outputs,
1284	                    request,
1285	                    request_id,
1286	                    self.state,
1287	                    finish_reasons,
1288	                )
1289	
1290	                if response is not None:
1291	                    if response.usage is not None:
1292	                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
1293	                            response.usage.extra = None
1294	                    yield response
1295	
1296	            suffix_response = engine_base.create_completion_suffix_response(
1297	                request, request_id, finish_reasons
1298	            )
1299	            if suffix_response is not None:
1300	                yield suffix_response
1301	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1302	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
1303	            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1304	            raise
1305	        except Exception as err:
1306	            logger.error("Error in _handle_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
1307	            raise

1309	    async def _generate(
1310	        self,
1311	        prompt: Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]],  # noqa: UP006
1312	        generation_config: GenerationConfig,
1313	        request_id: str,
1314	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput], Any]:  # noqa: UP006
1315	        """Internal asynchronous text generation interface of AsyncMLCEngine.
1316	        The method is a coroutine that streams a list of CallbackStreamOutput
1317	        at a time via yield. The returned list length is the number of
1318	        parallel generations specified by `generation_config.n`.
1319	
1320	        Parameters
1321	        ----------
1322	        prompt : Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]]
1323	            The input prompt in forms of text strings, lists of token ids or data.
1324	
1325	        generation_config : GenerationConfig
1326	            The generation config of the request.
1327	
1328	        request_id : str
1329	            The unique identifier (in string) or this generation request.
1330	
1331	        Yields
1332	        ------
1333	        request_output : List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]
1334	            The delta generated outputs in a list.
1335	            The number of list elements equals to `generation_config.n`,
1336	            and each element corresponds to the delta output of a parallel
1337	            generation.
1338	        """
1339	        if self._terminated:
1340	            raise ValueError("The AsyncThreadedEngine has terminated.")
1341	        self.state.async_lazy_init_event_loop()
1342	
1343	        # Create the request with the given id, input data, generation
1344	        # config and the created callback.
1345	        input_data = engine_utils.convert_prompts_to_data(prompt)
1346	        request = self._ffi["create_request"](
1347	            request_id, input_data, generation_config.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
1348	        )
1349	
1350	        # Create the unique async request stream of the request.
1351	        stream = engine_base.AsyncRequestStream()
1352	        if request_id in self.state.async_streamers:
1353	            # Report error in the stream if the request id already exists.
1354	            stream.push(
1355	                RuntimeError(
1356	                    f'The request id "{request_id} already exists. '
1357	                    'Please make sure the request id is unique."'
1358	                )
1359	            )
1360	        else:
1361	            # Record the stream in the tracker
1362	            self.state.async_streamers[request_id] = (
1363	                stream,
1364	                [TextStreamer(self.tokenizer) for _ in range(generation_config.n)],
1365	            )
1366	            self._ffi["add_request"](request)
1367	
1368	        def abort_request():
1369	            """clean up"""
1370	            self._abort(request_id)
1371	            logger.info("request %s cancelled", request_id)
1372	
1373	        with engine_utils.ErrorCleanupScope(abort_request):
1374	            # Iterate the stream asynchronously and yield the output.
1375	            try:
1376	                async for request_output in stream:
1377	                    yield request_output
1378	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
1379	                # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1380	                raise
1381	            except Exception as exception:
1382	                logger.error("Exception in _generate for request %s: %s", request_id, exception)
1383	                raise
1384	
1385	    def _abort(self, request_id: str):
1386	        """Internal implementation of request abortion."""
1387	        self.state.async_streamers.pop(request_id, None)
1388	        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)
1389

1391	class MLCEngine(engine_base.MLCEngineBase):
1392	    """The MLCEngine in MLC LLM that provides the synchronous
1393	    interfaces with regard to OpenAI API.
1394	
1395	    Parameters
1396	    ----------
1397	    model : str
1398	        A path to ``mlc-chat-config.json``, or an MLC model directory that contains
1399	        `mlc-chat-config.json`.
1400	        It can also be a link to a HF repository pointing to an MLC compiled model.
1401	
1402	    device: Union[str, Device]
1403	        The device used to deploy the model such as "cuda" or "cuda:0".
1404	        Will default to "auto" and detect from local available GPUs if not specified.
1405	
1406	    model_lib : Optional[str]
1407	        The full path to the model library file to use (e.g. a ``.so`` file).
1408	        If unspecified, we will use the provided ``model`` to search over possible paths.
1409	        It the model lib is not found, it will be compiled in a JIT manner.
1410	
1411	    mode : Literal["local", "interactive", "server"]
1412	        The engine mode in MLC LLM.
1413	        We provide three preset modes: "local", "interactive" and "server".
1414	        The default mode is "local".
1415	        The choice of mode decides the values of "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length"
1416	        and "prefill_chunk_size" when they are not explicitly specified.
1417	        1. Mode "local" refers to the local server deployment which has low
1418	        request concurrency. So the max batch size will be set to 4, and max
1419	        total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
1420	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
1421	        2. Mode "interactive" refers to the interactive use of server, which
1422	        has at most 1 concurrent request. So the max batch size will be set to 1,
1423	        and max total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
1424	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
1425	        3. Mode "server" refers to the large server use case which may handle
1426	        many concurrent request and want to use GPU memory as much as possible.
1427	        In this mode, we will automatically infer the largest possible max batch
1428	        size and max total sequence length.
1429	
1430	        You can manually specify arguments "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length" and
1431	        "prefill_chunk_size" to override the automatic inferred values.
1432	
1433	    engine_config : Optional[EngineConfig]
1434	        Additional configurable arguments of MLC engine.
1435	        See class "EngineConfig" for more detail.
1436	
1437	    enable_tracing : bool
1438	        A boolean indicating if to enable event logging for requests.
1439	    """
1440	
1441	    def __init__(
1442	        self,
1443	        model: str,
1444	        device: Union[str, Device] = "auto",
1445	        *,
1446	        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
1447	        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
1448	        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
1449	        enable_tracing: bool = False,
1450	    ) -> None:
1451	        super().__init__(
1452	            "sync",
1453	            model=model,
1454	            device=device,
1455	            model_lib=model_lib,
1456	            mode=mode,
1457	            engine_config=engine_config,
1458	            enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
1459	        )
1460	        self.chat = Chat(weakref.ref(self))
1461	        self.completions = Completion(weakref.ref(self))
1462	
1463	    def abort(self, request_id: str) -> None:
1464	        """Generation abortion interface.
1465	
1466	        Parameters
1467	        ---------
1468	        request_id : str
1469	            The id of the request to abort.
1470	        """
1471	        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)

1481	        return engine_base._query_engine_metrics(self)

1609	            tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
1610	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1611	            use_function_calling=use_function_calling,
1612	            usage=request_final_usage,
1613	        )
1614	
1615	    def _completion(
1616	        self,
1617	        *,
1618	        prompt: Union[str, List[int]],  # noqa: UP006
1619	        model: Optional[str] = None,
1620	        best_of: int = 1,
1621	        echo: bool = False,
1622	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
1623	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
1624	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
1625	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1626	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
1627	        n: int = 1,
1628	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
1629	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1630	        stream: bool = False,
1631	        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1632	        suffix: Optional[str] = None,
1633	        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
1634	        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
1635	        user: Optional[str] = None,
1636	        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1637	        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
1638	        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1639	    ) -> Union[
1640	        Iterator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse],
1641	        openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse,
1642	    ]:
1643	        """Synchronous completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
1644	
1645	        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create for specification.
1646	
1647	        Parameters
1648	        ----------
1649	        request_id : Optional[str]
1650	            The optional request id.
1651	            A random one will be generated if it is not given.
1652	
1653	        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
1654	            Extra debug options to pass to the request.
1655	
1656	        Raises
1657	        ------
1658	        e : BadRequestError
1659	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1660	        """
1661	        if request_id is None:
1662	            request_id = f"cmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
1663	
1664	        cmpl_generator = self._handle_completion(
1665	            openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest(
1666	                model=model,
1667	                prompt=prompt,
1668	                best_of=best_of,
1669	                echo=echo,
1670	                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
1671	                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
1672	                logprobs=logprobs,
1673	                logit_bias=logit_bias,
1674	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
1675	                n=n,
1676	                seed=seed,
1677	                stop=stop,
1678	                stream=stream,
1679	                stream_options=(
1680	                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
1681	                    if stream_options is not None
1682	                    else None
1683	                ),
1684	                suffix=suffix,
1685	                temperature=temperature,
1686	                top_p=top_p,
1687	                user=user,
1688	                response_format=(
1689	                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
1690	                    if response_format is not None
1691	                    else None
1692	                ),
1693	                debug_config=(
1694	                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
1695	                    if debug_config is not None
1696	                    else None
1697	                ),
1698	            ),
1699	            request_id=request_id,
1700	        )
1701	        if stream:
1702	            # Stream response.
1703	            return cmpl_generator
1704	        # Normal response.
1705	        request_final_usage = None
1706	        output_texts = [""] * n
1707	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006
1708	        logprob_results: List[Optional[openai_api_protocol.CompletionLogProbs]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006
1709	
1710	        for response in cmpl_generator:
1711	            # this is the final chunk
1712	            if response.usage is not None:
1713	                request_final_usage = response.usage
1714	                continue
1715	            for choice in response.choices:
1716	                output_texts[choice.index] += choice.text
1717	                if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
1718	                    finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
1719	                if choice.logprobs is not None:
1720	                    logprob_results[choice.index] = choice.logprobs
1721	
1722	        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
1723	        return engine_base.wrap_completion_response(
1724	            request_id=request_id,
1725	            model=model,
1726	            output_texts=output_texts,
1727	            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
1728	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1729	            usage=request_final_usage,
1730	        )
1731	
1732	    def _handle_chat_completion(
1733	        self, request: openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest, request_id: str
1734	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
1735	        """The implementation fo synchronous ChatCompletionRequest handling.
1736	
1737	        Yields
1738	        ------
1739	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1740	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1741	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1742	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.
1743	
1744	        Raises
1745	        ------
1746	        e : BadRequestError
1747	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1748	        """
1749	        (
1750	            prompts,
1751	            generation_cfg,
1752	            use_function_calling,
1753	            prompt_length,
1754	        ) = engine_base.process_chat_completion_request(
1755	            request,
1756	            request_id,
1757	            self.state,
1758	            self.model_config_dicts[0],
1759	            self.tokenizer.encode,
1760	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1761	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1762	        )
1763	        _ = prompt_length
1764	
1765	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1766	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1767	        for delta_outputs in self._generate(prompts, generation_cfg, request_id):
1768	            response = engine_base.process_chat_completion_stream_output(
1769	                delta_outputs,
1770	                request,
1771	                request_id,
1772	                self.state,
1773	                use_function_calling,
1774	                finish_reasons,
1775	            )
1776	            if response is not None:
1777	                yield response
1778	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1779	
1780	    def _handle_completion(
1781	        self, request: openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest, request_id: str
1782	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse]:
1783	        """The implementation for synchronous CompletionRequest handling.
1784	
1785	        Yields
1786	        ------
1787	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1788	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1789	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1790	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/object for specification.
1791	
1792	        Raises
1793	        ------
1794	        e : BadRequestError
1795	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1796	        """
1797	        (
1798	            prompt,
1799	            generation_cfg,
1800	            prompt_length,
1801	            echo_response,
1802	        ) = engine_base.process_completion_request(
1803	            request,
1804	            request_id,
1805	            self.state,
1806	            self.tokenizer,
1807	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1808	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1809	        )
1810	        _ = prompt_length
1811	        if echo_response is not None:
1812	            yield echo_response
1813	
1814	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1815	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1816	        for delta_outputs in self._generate(prompt, generation_cfg, request_id):
1817	            response = engine_base.process_completion_stream_output(
1818	                delta_outputs,
1819	                request,
1820	                request_id,
1821	                self.state,
1822	                finish_reasons,
1823	            )
1824	            if response is not None:
1825	                yield response
1826	
1827	        suffix_response = engine_base.create_completion_suffix_response(
1828	            request, request_id, finish_reasons
1829	        )
1830	        if suffix_response is not None:
1831	            yield suffix_response
1832	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1833	
1834	    def _generate(
1835	        self,
1836	        prompt: Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]],  # noqa: UP006
1837	        generation_config: GenerationConfig,
1838	        request_id: str,
1839	    ) -> Iterator[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]]:  # noqa: UP006
1840	        """Internal synchronous text generation interface of MLCEngine.
1841	        The method is a coroutine that streams a list of CallbackStreamOutput
1842	        at a time via yield. The returned list length is the number of
1843	        parallel generations specified by `generation_config.n`
1844	        except for the final chunk(which is always an List of size 1 and comes with usage)
1845	
1846	        Parameters
1847	        ----------
1848	        prompt : Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]]
1849	            The input prompt in forms of text strings, lists of token ids or data.
1850	
1851	        generation_config : GenerationConfig
1852	            The generation config of the request.
1853	
1854	        request_id : str
1855	            The unique identifier (in string) or this generation request.
1856	
1857	        Yields
1858	        ------
1859	        request_output : List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]
1860	            The delta generated outputs in a list.
1861	            Except for the final chunk, the number of list elements equals to `generation_config.n`,
1862	            and each element corresponds to the delta output of a parallel generation.
1863	        """
1864	        if self._terminated:
1865	            raise ValueError("The engine has terminated.")
1866	
1867	        # Create the request with the given id, input data, generation
1868	        # config and the created callback.
1869	        input_data = engine_utils.convert_prompts_to_data(prompt)
1870	        request = self._ffi["create_request"](
1871	            request_id, input_data, generation_config.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
1872	        )
1873	
1874	        # Record the stream in the tracker
1875	        self.state.sync_output_queue = queue.Queue()
1876	        self.state.sync_text_streamers = [
1877	            TextStreamer(self.tokenizer) for _ in range(generation_config.n)
1878	        ]
1879	        self._ffi["add_request"](request)
1880	
1881	        def abort_request():
1882	            """clean up request if exception happens"""
1883	            self.abort(request_id)
1884	
1885	        # Iterate the stream asynchronously and yield the token.
1886	        with engine_utils.ErrorCleanupScope(abort_request):
1887	            while True:
1888	                delta_outputs = self.state.sync_output_queue.get()
1889	                request_outputs, request_final_usage_json_str = self._request_stream_callback_impl(
1890	                    delta_outputs
1891	                )
1892	                for request_output in request_outputs:
1893	                    yield request_output
1894	
1895	                if request_final_usage_json_str is not None:
1896	                    # final chunk, we can break
1897	                    output = engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput(
1898	                        delta_text="",
1899	                        delta_logprob_json_strs=None,
1900	                        finish_reason=None,
1901	                        request_final_usage_json_str=request_final_usage_json_str,
1902	                    )
1903	                    yield [output]
1904	                    break
1905	
1906	    def _request_stream_callback_impl(
1907	        self,
1908	        delta_outputs: List[data.RequestStreamOutput],  # noqa: UP006
1909	    ) -> Tuple[List[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]], Optional[str]]:  # noqa: UP006
1910	        """The underlying implementation of request stream callback of MLCEngine."""
1911	        batch_outputs: List[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]] = []  # noqa: UP006
1912	        for delta_output in delta_outputs:
1913	            request_id, stream_outputs = delta_output.unpack()
1914	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="start callback")
1915	
1916	            # final chunk is now always indicated by a chunk
1917	            # where usage json is present
1918	            # the backend engine always streams back this chunk
1919	            # regardless of include_usage option
1920	            is_final_chunk = stream_outputs[0].request_final_usage_json_str is not None
1921	            if is_final_chunk:
1922	                return (batch_outputs, stream_outputs[0].request_final_usage_json_str)
1923	
1924	            outputs: List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput] = []  # noqa: UP006
1925	            for stream_output, text_streamer in zip(stream_outputs, self.state.sync_text_streamers):
1926	                self.state.record_event(request_id, event="start detokenization")
1927	                delta_text = stream_output.extra_prefix_string + (
1928	                    text_streamer.put(stream_output.delta_token_ids)
1929	                    if len(stream_output.delta_token_ids) > 0
1930	                    else ""
1931	                )
1932	                if stream_output.finish_reason is not None:
1933	                    delta_text += text_streamer.finish()
1934	                self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish detokenization")
1935	
1936	                outputs.append(
1937	                    engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput(
1938	                        delta_text=delta_text,
1939	                        delta_logprob_json_strs=stream_output.delta_logprob_json_strs,
1940	                        finish_reason=stream_output.finish_reason,
1941	                        request_final_usage_json_str=None,
1942	                    )
1943	                )
1944	            batch_outputs.append(outputs)
1945	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish callback")
1946	        return (batch_outputs, None)
1947

"""The MLC LLM Serving Engine."""

import asyncio
import queue
import weakref
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator, Iterator
from typing import (  # noqa: UP035
    Any,
    Dict,
    List,
    Literal,
    Optional,
    Tuple,
    Union,
    overload,
)

from tvm.runtime import Device

from mlc_llm.protocol import debug_protocol, openai_api_protocol
from mlc_llm.protocol.generation_config import GenerationConfig
from mlc_llm.serve import data, engine_utils
from mlc_llm.serve.config import EngineConfig
from mlc_llm.support import logging
from mlc_llm.tokenizers import TextStreamer

from . import engine_base

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# Note: we define both AsyncChat and Chat for Python type analysis.
class AsyncChat:
    """The proxy class to direct to async chat completions."""

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        assert isinstance(engine(), AsyncMLCEngine)
        self.completions = AsyncChatCompletion(engine)


class Chat:
    """The proxy class to direct to chat completions."""

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        assert isinstance(engine(), MLCEngine)
        self.completions = ChatCompletion(engine)


class AsyncChatCompletion:
    """The proxy class to direct to async chat completions."""

    engine: weakref.ReferenceType["AsyncMLCEngine"]

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        self.engine = engine

    @overload
    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        stream: Literal[True],
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any]:
        """Asynchronous streaming chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
        The method is a coroutine that streams ChatCompletionStreamResponse
        that conforms to OpenAI API one at a time via yield.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Yields
        ------
        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """

    @overload
    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: Literal[False] = False,
        stream_options: Literal[None] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse:
        """Asynchronous non-streaming chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
        The method is a coroutine that streams ChatCompletionStreamResponse
        that conforms to OpenAI API one at a time via yield.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Returns
        -------
        response : ChatCompletionResponse
            The chat completion response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/object for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """

    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = False,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Union[
        AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any],
        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse,
    ]:
        """Asynchronous chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        return await self.engine()._chat_completion(
            messages=messages,
            model=model,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            n=n,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stream=stream,
            stream_options=(
                openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                if stream_options is not None
                else None
            ),
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            tools=tools,
            tool_choice=tool_choice,
            user=user,
            response_format=response_format,
            request_id=request_id,
            debug_config=(extra_body.get("debug_config", None) if extra_body is not None else None),
        )


class ChatCompletion:
    """The proxy class to direct to chat completions."""

    engine: weakref.ReferenceType["MLCEngine"]

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        self.engine = engine

    @overload
    def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        stream: Literal[True],
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
        """Synchronous streaming chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
        The method streams back ChatCompletionStreamResponse that conforms to
        OpenAI API one at a time via yield.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Yields
        ------
        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.

class AsyncMLCEngine(engine_base.MLCEngineBase):
    """The AsyncMLCEngine in MLC LLM that provides the asynchronous
    interfaces with regard to OpenAI API.

    Parameters
    ----------
    model : str
        A path to ``mlc-chat-config.json``, or an MLC model directory that contains
        `mlc-chat-config.json`.
        It can also be a link to a HF repository pointing to an MLC compiled model.

    device: Union[str, Device]
        The device used to deploy the model such as "cuda" or "cuda:0".
        Will default to "auto" and detect from local available GPUs if not specified.

    model_lib : Optional[str]
        The full path to the model library file to use (e.g. a ``.so`` file).
        If unspecified, we will use the provided ``model`` to search over possible paths.
        It the model lib is not found, it will be compiled in a JIT manner.

    mode : Literal["local", "interactive", "server"]
        The engine mode in MLC LLM.
        We provide three preset modes: "local", "interactive" and "server".
        The default mode is "local".
        The choice of mode decides the values of "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length"
        and "prefill_chunk_size" when they are not explicitly specified.
        1. Mode "local" refers to the local server deployment which has low
        request concurrency. So the max batch size will be set to 4, and max
        total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
        2. Mode "interactive" refers to the interactive use of server, which
        has at most 1 concurrent request. So the max batch size will be set to 1,
        and max total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
        3. Mode "server" refers to the large server use case which may handle
        many concurrent request and want to use GPU memory as much as possible.
        In this mode, we will automatically infer the largest possible max batch
        size and max total sequence length.

        You can manually specify arguments "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length" and
        "prefill_chunk_size" to override the automatic inferred values.

    engine_config : Optional[EngineConfig]
        Additional configurable arguments of MLC engine.
        See class "EngineConfig" for more detail.

    enable_tracing : bool
        A boolean indicating if to enable event logging for requests.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: str,
        device: Union[str, Device] = "auto",
        *,
        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
        enable_tracing: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(
            "async",
            model=model,
            device=device,
            model_lib=model_lib,
            mode=mode,
            engine_config=engine_config,
            enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
        )
        self.chat = AsyncChat(weakref.ref(self))
        self.completions = AsyncCompletion(weakref.ref(self))

    async def abort(self, request_id: str) -> None:
        """Generation abortion interface.

        Parameters
        ---------
        request_id : str
            The id of the request to abort.
        """
        self._abort(request_id)

    async def metrics(self) -> engine_base.EngineMetrics:
        """Get engine metrics

        Returns
        -------
        metrics: EngineMetrics
            The engine metrics
        """
        return await engine_base._async_query_engine_metrics(self)

    async def _chat_completion(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = False,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Union[
        AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any],
        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse,
    ]:
        """Asynchronous chat completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Debug config body options to pass to the request.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        if request_id is None:
            request_id = f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"

        chatcmpl_generator = self._handle_chat_completion(
            openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest(
                messages=[
                    openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
                    for message in messages
                ],
                model=model,
                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
                logprobs=logprobs,
                top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
                logit_bias=logit_bias,
                max_tokens=max_tokens,
                n=n,
                seed=seed,
                stop=stop,
                stream=stream,
                stream_options=(
                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                    if stream_options is not None
                    else None
                ),
                temperature=temperature,
                top_p=top_p,
                tools=(
                    [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
                    if tools is not None
                    else None
                ),
                tool_choice=tool_choice,
                user=user,
                response_format=(
                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
                    if response_format is not None
                    else None
                ),
                debug_config=(
                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
                    if debug_config is not None
                    else None
                ),
            ),
            request_id=request_id,
            request_final_usage_include_extra=True,
        )
        if stream:
            # Stream response.
            return chatcmpl_generator
        # Normal response.
        output_texts = ["" for _ in range(n)]
        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(n)]  # noqa: UP006
        logprob_results: Optional[List[List[openai_api_protocol.LogProbsContent]]] = (  # noqa: UP006
            [[] for _ in range(n)] if logprobs else None
        )
        request_final_usage = None
        try:
            async for response in chatcmpl_generator:
                # when usage is not None this is the last chunk
                if response.usage is not None:
                    request_final_usage = response.usage
                    continue
                for choice in response.choices:
                    assert isinstance(choice.delta.content, str)
                    output_texts[choice.index] += choice.delta.content
                    if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
                        finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
                    if choice.logprobs is not None:
                        assert logprob_results is not None
                        logprob_results[choice.index] += choice.logprobs.content
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
            raise
        except Exception as err:
            logger.error("Error in chat completion with request ID %s: %s", request_id, err)
            raise

        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
        use_function_calling, tool_calls_list = engine_base.process_function_call_output(
            output_texts, finish_reasons
        )
        return engine_base.wrap_chat_completion_response(
            request_id=request_id,
            model=model,
            output_texts=output_texts,
            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
            tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
            logprob_results=logprob_results,
            use_function_calling=use_function_calling,
            usage=request_final_usage,
        )

    async def _completion(
        self,
        *,
        prompt: Union[str, List[int]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        best_of: int = 1,
        echo: bool = False,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = False,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        suffix: Optional[str] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Union[
        AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse, Any],
        openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse,
    ]:
        """Asynchronous completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra debug options to pass to the request.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        if request_id is None:
            request_id = f"cmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
        cmpl_generator = self._handle_completion(
            openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest(
                model=model,
                prompt=prompt,
                best_of=best_of,
                echo=echo,
                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
                logprobs=logprobs,
                logit_bias=logit_bias,
                max_tokens=max_tokens,
                n=n,
                seed=seed,
                stop=stop,
                stream=stream,
                stream_options=(
                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                    if stream_options is not None
                    else None
                ),
                suffix=suffix,
                temperature=temperature,
                top_p=top_p,
                user=user,
                response_format=(
                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
                    if response_format is not None
                    else None
                ),
                debug_config=(
                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
                    if debug_config is not None
                    else None
                ),
            ),
            request_id=request_id,
            request_final_usage_include_extra=True,
        )
        if stream:
            # Stream response.
            return cmpl_generator
        # Normal response.
        request_final_usage = None
        output_texts = [""] * n
        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006
        logprob_results: List[Optional[openai_api_protocol.CompletionLogProbs]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006

        async for response in cmpl_generator:
            # this is the final chunk
            if response.usage is not None:
                request_final_usage = response.usage
                continue
            for choice in response.choices:
                output_texts[choice.index] += choice.text
                if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
                    finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
                if choice.logprobs is not None:
                    logprob_results[choice.index] = choice.logprobs

        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)

        return engine_base.wrap_completion_response(
            request_id=request_id,
            model=model,
            output_texts=output_texts,
            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
            logprob_results=logprob_results,
            usage=request_final_usage,
        )

    async def _handle_chat_completion(
        self,
        request: openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest,
        request_id: str,
        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any]:
        """The implementation fo asynchronous ChatCompletionRequest handling.

        Yields
        ------
        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        (
            prompts,
            generation_cfg,
            use_function_calling,
            prompt_length,
        ) = engine_base.process_chat_completion_request(
            request,
            request_id,
            self.state,
            self.model_config_dicts[0],
            self.tokenizer.encode,
            self.max_input_sequence_length,
            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
        )
        # prompt length is not used
        _ = prompt_length
        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
        try:
            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
                prompts,
                generation_cfg,
                request_id,
            ):
                response = engine_base.process_chat_completion_stream_output(
                    delta_outputs,
                    request,
                    request_id,
                    self.state,
                    use_function_calling,
                    finish_reasons,
                )

                if response is not None:
                    if response.usage is not None:
                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
                            response.usage.extra = None
                    yield response
            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
            raise
        except Exception as err:
            logger.error("Error in _handle_chat_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
            raise

    async def _handle_completion(
        self,
        request: openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest,
        request_id: str,
        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse, Any]:
        """The implementation fo asynchronous CompletionRequest handling.

        Yields
        ------
        stream_response : CompletionResponse
            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/object for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        (
            prompt,
            generation_cfg,
            prompt_length,
            echo_response,
        ) = engine_base.process_completion_request(
            request,
            request_id,
            self.state,
            self.tokenizer,
            self.max_input_sequence_length,
            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
        )
        _ = prompt_length
        if echo_response is not None:
            yield echo_response

        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * generation_cfg.n  # noqa: UP006
        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
        try:
            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
                prompt,
                generation_cfg,
                request_id,
            ):
                response = engine_base.process_completion_stream_output(
                    delta_outputs,
                    request,
                    request_id,
                    self.state,
                    finish_reasons,
                )

                if response is not None:
                    if response.usage is not None:
                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
                            response.usage.extra = None
                    yield response

            suffix_response = engine_base.create_completion_suffix_response(
                request, request_id, finish_reasons
            )
            if suffix_response is not None:
                yield suffix_response
            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
            raise
        except Exception as err:
            logger.error("Error in _handle_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
            raise

    async def _generate(
        self,
        prompt: Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]],  # noqa: UP006
        generation_config: GenerationConfig,
        request_id: str,
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput], Any]:  # noqa: UP006
        """Internal asynchronous text generation interface of AsyncMLCEngine.
        The method is a coroutine that streams a list of CallbackStreamOutput
        at a time via yield. The returned list length is the number of
        parallel generations specified by `generation_config.n`.

        Parameters
        ----------
        prompt : Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]]
            The input prompt in forms of text strings, lists of token ids or data.

        generation_config : GenerationConfig
            The generation config of the request.

        request_id : str
            The unique identifier (in string) or this generation request.

        Yields
        ------
        request_output : List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]
            The delta generated outputs in a list.
            The number of list elements equals to `generation_config.n`,
            and each element corresponds to the delta output of a parallel
            generation.
        """
        if self._terminated:
            raise ValueError("The AsyncThreadedEngine has terminated.")
        self.state.async_lazy_init_event_loop()

        # Create the request with the given id, input data, generation
        # config and the created callback.
        input_data = engine_utils.convert_prompts_to_data(prompt)
        request = self._ffi["create_request"](
            request_id, input_data, generation_config.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
        )

        # Create the unique async request stream of the request.
        stream = engine_base.AsyncRequestStream()
        if request_id in self.state.async_streamers:
            # Report error in the stream if the request id already exists.
            stream.push(
                RuntimeError(
                    f'The request id "{request_id} already exists. '
                    'Please make sure the request id is unique."'
                )
            )
        else:
            # Record the stream in the tracker
            self.state.async_streamers[request_id] = (
                stream,
                [TextStreamer(self.tokenizer) for _ in range(generation_config.n)],
            )
            self._ffi["add_request"](request)

        def abort_request():
            """clean up"""
            self._abort(request_id)
            logger.info("request %s cancelled", request_id)

        with engine_utils.ErrorCleanupScope(abort_request):
            # Iterate the stream asynchronously and yield the output.
            try:
                async for request_output in stream:
                    yield request_output
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
                raise
            except Exception as exception:
                logger.error("Exception in _generate for request %s: %s", request_id, exception)
                raise

    def _abort(self, request_id: str):
        """Internal implementation of request abortion."""
        self.state.async_streamers.pop(request_id, None)
        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)


class MLCEngine(engine_base.MLCEngineBase):
    """The MLCEngine in MLC LLM that provides the synchronous
    interfaces with regard to OpenAI API.

    Parameters
    ----------
    model : str
        A path to ``mlc-chat-config.json``, or an MLC model directory that contains
        `mlc-chat-config.json`.
        It can also be a link to a HF repository pointing to an MLC compiled model.

    device: Union[str, Device]
        The device used to deploy the model such as "cuda" or "cuda:0".
        Will default to "auto" and detect from local available GPUs if not specified.

    model_lib : Optional[str]
        The full path to the model library file to use (e.g. a ``.so`` file).
        If unspecified, we will use the provided ``model`` to search over possible paths.
        It the model lib is not found, it will be compiled in a JIT manner.

    mode : Literal["local", "interactive", "server"]
        The engine mode in MLC LLM.
        We provide three preset modes: "local", "interactive" and "server".
        The default mode is "local".
        The choice of mode decides the values of "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length"
        and "prefill_chunk_size" when they are not explicitly specified.
        1. Mode "local" refers to the local server deployment which has low
        request concurrency. So the max batch size will be set to 4, and max
        total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
        2. Mode "interactive" refers to the interactive use of server, which
        has at most 1 concurrent request. So the max batch size will be set to 1,
        and max total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
        3. Mode "server" refers to the large server use case which may handle
        many concurrent request and want to use GPU memory as much as possible.
        In this mode, we will automatically infer the largest possible max batch
        size and max total sequence length.

        You can manually specify arguments "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length" and
        "prefill_chunk_size" to override the automatic inferred values.

    engine_config : Optional[EngineConfig]
        Additional configurable arguments of MLC engine.
        See class "EngineConfig" for more detail.

    enable_tracing : bool
        A boolean indicating if to enable event logging for requests.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: str,
        device: Union[str, Device] = "auto",
        *,
        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
        enable_tracing: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(
            "sync",
            model=model,
            device=device,
            model_lib=model_lib,
            mode=mode,
            engine_config=engine_config,
            enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
        )
        self.chat = Chat(weakref.ref(self))
        self.completions = Completion(weakref.ref(self))

    def abort(self, request_id: str) -> None:
        """Generation abortion interface.

        Parameters
        ---------
        request_id : str
            The id of the request to abort.
        """
        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)

    def metrics(self) -> engine_base.EngineMetrics:
        """Get engine metrics

        Returns
        -------
        metrics: EngineMetrics
            The engine metrics
        """
        return engine_base._query_engine_metrics(self)

    def _chat_completion(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = False,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Union[
        Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse],
        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse,
    ]:
        """Synchronous chat completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra debug options to pass to the request.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """
        if request_id is None:
            request_id = f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"

        chatcmpl_generator = self._handle_chat_completion(
            openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest(
                messages=[
                    openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
                    for message in messages
                ],
                model=model,
                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
                logprobs=logprobs,
                top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
                logit_bias=logit_bias,
                max_tokens=max_tokens,
                n=n,
                seed=seed,
                stop=stop,
                stream=stream,
                stream_options=(
                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                    if stream_options is not None
                    else None
                ),
                temperature=temperature,
                top_p=top_p,
                tools=(
                    [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
                    if tools is not None
                    else None
                ),
                tool_choice=tool_choice,
                user=user,
                response_format=(
                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
                    if response_format is not None
                    else None
                ),
                debug_config=(
                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
                    if debug_config is not None
                    else None
                ),
            ),
            request_id=request_id,
        )
        if stream:
            # Stream response.
            return chatcmpl_generator
        # Normal response.
        request_final_usage = None
        output_texts = ["" for _ in range(n)]
        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(n)]  # noqa: UP006
        logprob_results: Optional[List[List[openai_api_protocol.LogProbsContent]]] = (  # noqa: UP006
            [[] for _ in range(n)] if logprobs else None
        )
        for response in chatcmpl_generator:
            # if usage is not None, this is the last chunk
            if response.usage is not None:
                request_final_usage = response.usage
                continue
            for choice in response.choices:
                assert isinstance(choice.delta.content, str)
                output_texts[choice.index] += choice.delta.content
                if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
                    finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
                if choice.logprobs is not None:
                    assert logprob_results is not None
                    logprob_results[choice.index] += choice.logprobs.content

        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
        use_function_calling, tool_calls_list = engine_base.process_function_call_output(
            output_texts, finish_reasons
        )
        return engine_base.wrap_chat_completion_response(
            request_id=request_id,
            model=model,
            output_texts=output_texts,
            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
            tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
            logprob_results=logprob_results,
            use_function_calling=use_function_calling,
            usage=request_final_usage,
        )

    def _completion(
        self,
        *,
        prompt: Union[str, List[int]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        best_of: int = 1,
        echo: bool = False,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006

    ) -> Union[
        Iterator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse],

        """Synchronous completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.

        Parameters
        ----------

            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        """

                stream=stream,

                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                    if stream_options is not None
                    else None
                ),
